task.name: Observability smoke test (consolidated)
task.tdd_spec: RED: 3 parametrized test cases, all fail (module not yet smoke-tested). GREEN: If context managers already produce events.jsonl (Pass 3 wiring), tests pass. If not, fix the context managers.

task.name: Real trainer behavioral test (epoch_complete events)
task.description:
DevOps suggestion #2: NOT a mock test — real SegmentationTrainer.fit(). @pytest.mark.model_loading (prod tier only — loads DynUNet).
Creates tiny DynUNet (32 filters, 2 layers), 2 synthetic 32x32x32 volumes, runs 2 epochs via trainer.fit(). After completion, verifies: - events.jsonl has exactly 2 epoch_complete entries - Each entry has: train_loss, epoch, epoch_wall_s - Epoch numbers are monotonically increasing (1, 2)
This is the ONLY test that catches "trainer.fit() never calls log_epoch_complete()."
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test expects 2 epoch_complete entries — currently may be 0. GREEN: Verify trainer.fit() calls log_epoch_complete() in epoch loop.

task.name: Thread-local event_logger transfer test
task.description:
DevOps MAJOR gap: train_flow.py uses thread-local storage for event_logger. If Prefect ever runs tasks in separate threads, the reference is lost.
Test: 1. Set _flow_context.event_logger in one function 2. Call a function that reads _flow_context.event_logger 3. Verify the reference survived (same object) 4. Also test the FAILURE case: new thread → reference is None
This documents the in-process execution assumption.
task.tdd_spec: RED: Test thread-local survives same-thread call. RED: Test thread-local is None in new thread. GREEN: Both should pass (documenting current behavior).

task.name: Healthcheck exit code verification
task.description:
Test healthcheck_training.py and healthcheck_cpu.py exit codes: - Fresh heartbeat.json (less than 60s old) → exit 0 - Missing heartbeat.json → exit 1 - Stale heartbeat.json (older than 120s) → exit 1 - Fresh events.jsonl → exit 0 (CPU flows)
Mock /proc/1/stat via monkeypatch for container uptime.
task.tdd_spec: RED: 4 test cases for exit codes. GREEN: Verify or fix healthcheck scripts.

task.name: Docker monitoring rule (decision tree format)
task.description:
ALREADY CREATED at .claude/rules/docker-monitoring.md. Verify it loads correctly and is referenced by Claude Code.
DevOps suggestion #6: decision tree format, not sequential. The rule file should cover: - "Container running but no output" → check entrypoint, check imports - "Container exited code 1" → read docker logs, check ModuleNotFoundError - "Container shows unhealthy" → check healthcheck script, check file freshness - "Container OOM killed (137)" → reduce batch_size, check --shm-size

task.name: Makefile target: make verify-observability
task.description:
DevOps suggestion #7: single command that answers "is observability working?"
Runs: 1. Behavioral smoke tests (Phase 1) 2. AST enforcement tests (existing) 3. docker compose config validation (healthcheck stanzas present)
Target: verify-observability in Makefile

task.name: Run biostatistics flow with observability verification
task.description:
Run biostatistics flow (CPU, fast) in Docker and verify: 1. docker logs shows structured JSONL 2. events.jsonl has flow_start + flow_end 3. Docker HEALTHCHECK shows "healthy" 4. Output DuckDB files exist
Use the monitoring playbook from .claude/rules/docker-monitoring.md. FOLLOW EVERY STEP — no ad-hoc monitoring.

task.name: Run training flow mini-test with GPU observability
task.description:
Run train flow with 1 epoch, 4 volumes (minimal GPU) and verify: 1. heartbeat.json updates 2. events.jsonl has epoch_complete 3. CUDA guard passes 4. Docker HEALTHCHECK shows "healthy"
This is the capstone — if this passes, observability is PROVEN working.

dependency.reason: GPU test after CPU test succeeds
